'''BioWare''' is an electronic entertainment company best known for its computer [[roleplaying game]]s. It is based in [[Wikipedia:Edmonton|Edmonton]], [[Wikipedia:Alberta|Alberta]], [[Wikipedia:Canada|Canada]], with a second studio in [[Wikipedia:Austin, Texas|Austin]], [[Wikipedia:Texas|Texas]], [[Wikipedia:United States of America|USA]], and a third in [[Wikipedia:Montreal, Quebec|Montreal]], [[Wikipedia:Quebec|Quebec]], Canada.

==History==
BioWare's first ''Star Wars'' game was ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', released for both Microsoft [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] and PC in 2003.

On [[November 3]], [[2005]], [[Wikipedia:Elevation Partners|Elevation Partners]], a [[Wikipedia:Private equity|private equity]] firm, announced the creation of a partnership between BioWare and [[Pandemic Studios]], the developers of ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'' and its sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''. Though they now have common ownership and pool their expertise to develop games, both companies retain their individual staffs and brands.<ref name="BioWare Pandemic">{{WebCite|url=http://bioware.pandemicstudios.com|text=Elevation Partners' First Investment Creates BioWare, Pandemic Partnership|date=2005-11-03|work=bioware.pandemicstudios.com|archivedate=20051201010446}}</ref> On [[October 11]], [[2007]], [[Electronic Arts]] announced they had purchased both studios from Elevation Partners.<ref name="EA acquisition" />

Years of rumors and wishful thinking by fans surrounded the possibility of a BioWare-produced ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic 3]]'' or some other ''Star Wars'' project. BioWare's spokespeople typically responded to these rumors by stating their intention to focus on original titles (such as [[W:c:jadeempire:Jade Empire (Xbox)|''Jade Empire'']], [[W:c:masseffect:Mass Effect|''Mass Effect'']], and [[W:c:dragonage:Dragon Age: Origins|''Dragon Age: Origins'']]) rather than licensed games. However, in 2007, a LucasArts-BioWare partnership to produce a "ground-breaking interactive entertainment product" was announced, though details were vague.<ref name="lucasartsbioware">{{WebCite|url=http://www.lucasartsbioware.com|text=Lucasarts and Bioware Corp. To Develop Ground-Breaking Interactive Entertainment Product|work=lucasartsbioware.com|archivedate=20071031032648}}</ref>

In the following months, rumors persisted about the development of a new ''Knights of the Old Republic'' video game, spurred on by leaks from within BioWare<ref name="BioWare's Upcoming MMO Based in KOTOR Universe">{{WebCite|url=http://mmolab.com/2007/10/30/bioware%E2%80%99s-upcoming-mmo-based-in-kotor-universe|text=BioWare's Upcoming MMO Based in KOTOR Universe|author=erazor|date=2007-10-30|work=the mmolab|archivedate=20071214202657}}</ref> and LucasArts<ref name="Ex-Lucasarts Employee claims BioWare MMO is Kotor3">{{WebCite|url=http://mmolab.com/2008/06/08/ex-lucasarts-employee-claims-bioware-mmo-is-kotor3|text=Ex-Lucasarts Employee claims BioWare MMO is Kotor3|date=2008-06-08|work=the mmolab|archivedate=20080829000127}}</ref> and the registration of "Star Wars The Old Republic"<ref name="trademark1">{{WebCite|url=https://tsdr.uspto.gov/#caseNumber=77514507&caseType=SERIAL_NO&searchType=statusSearch|text=STAR WARS THE OLD REPUBLIC|work=United States Patent and Trademark Office|archivedate=2012-12-14|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/JG15}}</ref> and "The Old Republic"<ref name="trademark2">{{WebCite|url=https://tsdr.uspto.gov/#caseNumber=77514514&caseType=SERIAL_NO&searchType=statusSearch|text=THE OLD REPUBLIC|work=United States Patent and Trademark Office|archivedate=2012-12-14|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/vn6m}}</ref> as LucasArts trademarks. During the [[Wikipedia:E3|E3]] [[2008]] expo, Electronic Arts executive John Riccitello confirmed that the game ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' would be connected to the ''Knights of the Old Republic'' series of video games.<ref name="EACEOconfirm">{{WebCite|url=http://www.portfolio.com/views/blogs/the-tech-observer/2008/07/17/ea-to-play-in-knights-of-the-old-republic|text=EA to Play in Knights of the Old Republic|date=2008-07-17|work=Portfolio.com|archivedate=20080719041900}}</ref> It was released in North America and Europe on [[December 20]], [[2011]].

In June [[2023]], BioWare handed off maintenance and further development of ''Star Wars: The Old Republic'' to [[Broadsword Online Games]] after almost 12 years under BioWare. The majority of the ''Old Republic'' development team at BioWare was intended to join Broadsword to continue to work on the game.<ref name="Gamespot">{{GameSpot|url=articles/bioware-is-officially-handing-off-development-of-star-wars-the-old-republic/1100-6515522|text=BioWare Is Officially Handing Off Development Of Star Wars: The Old Republic}}</ref><ref name="TOR">{{TORweb|url=info/news/article/20230627|text=Studio Update - ''Star Wars''™: The Old Republic™}}</ref><ref name="bioware">{{WebCite|url=https://blog.bioware.com/2023/06/27/bright-future-for-swtor|text=The Future is Bright for Star Wars™: The Old Republic™|archivedate=20230627160446}}</ref>
